name: Tookitaki
tagline: SAS wins on enterprise maturity, platform breadth, and proven Philippine deployments

overview.whatTheyDo: Singapore-based AML startup offering federated learning-based financial crime detection. Unique 'Anti-Financial Crime (AFC) Ecosystem' uses shared typology models across institutions without sharing raw data. Focuses on AML transaction monitoring and screening.
overview.sweetSpot: Mid-size banks in Southeast Asia wanting AML with collaborative intelligence. Appeals to banks wanting 'modern AML' without the cost of NICE or SAS. Strong narrative around shared learning.
overview.typicalBuyer: Head of Compliance, AMLO, Innovation/Digital Transformation leads at mid-size banks
overview.phPresence: Small but growing. Singapore headquarters with ASEAN focus. Some exploratory conversations with Philippine banks and thrift banks. Limited brand recognition among PH compliance decision-makers.
overview.recentMomentum: Series B funding, AFC Ecosystem expansion, partnerships with ASEAN banks, regulatory sandbox participation in Singapore, expanding from AML into fraud detection.
overview.pricingModel: SaaS subscription based on entity count and transaction volume. Competitive pricing for mid-market — significantly below NICE/SAS list price. Community pricing model for AFC Ecosystem participants.

whereWeWin.headline: Enterprise Maturity and Proven Scale
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS has 50 years of deployment at the world's largest banks and national-scale implementations. Tookitaki is a startup with limited enterprise-scale production references. For BSP-regulated universal banks, proven stability matters more than innovative concepts.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS deployed at 3,000+ financial institutions globally, processing national-scale transaction volumes. Tookitaki's largest deployments are mid-size banks with limited transaction volumes.

whereWeWin.headline: Complete Platform (Not Just AML)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS provides fraud, AML, credit risk, model governance, and analytics on one platform. Tookitaki is AML-focused with limited fraud capabilities and no risk or analytics platform. Philippine banks need BSP 1213 (fraud) AND AMLA (AML) — SAS covers both.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: BSP Circular 1213 requires fraud management systems by June 2026. Tookitaki addresses AML but not the fraud management requirement — leaving a critical gap.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Tookitaki's AFC Ecosystem allows banks to benefit from shared AML patterns without sharing raw data. This collaborative approach theoretically improves detection across the network. Innovative concept that resonates with forward-thinking compliance officers.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Federated learning is innovative but unproven at scale. Ask: how many Philippine banks participate? What's the actual detection improvement? SAS's own ML algorithms deliver comparable improvements using your own data — no dependency on other banks' participation.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Tookitaki's pricing is significantly below SAS for smaller banks. A thrift bank or rural bank may find Tookitaki affordable where SAS pricing is prohibitive.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: For universal banks (SAS's target), the cost comparison is misleading — Tookitaki + separate fraud + separate analytics + governance tools = more expensive. Focus on universal banks where SAS's breadth delivers TCO advantage. Don't compete for small thrift banks.

features.notes: Both provide AML; SAS deeper at enterprise scale
SAS full fraud suite; Tookitaki basic fraud, primary focus is AML
Tookitaki's unique AFC Ecosystem; SAS supports consortium architecturally
Both ML-based; SAS broader algorithm library and model management
SAS full network analytics; Tookitaki limited
SAS proven BSP reporting; Tookitaki limited PH-specific templates
SAS full platform; Tookitaki is AML point solution
SAS Model Manager; Tookitaki has basic model documentation
Both have case management for compliance workflows

landmines.question: How many Philippine banks are currently participating in the AFC Ecosystem, and what measurable detection improvement have they seen?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Very few or no PH banks currently participate — ecosystem benefits are theoretical in this market
landmines.whatToSayNext: Shared learning requires critical mass of participants. Without Philippine bank participation, the ecosystem benefit doesn't apply here. SAS's own ML delivers proven 50-70% false positive reduction using your own data.

landmines.question: BSP Circular 1213 requires a fraud management system by June 2026. How does this platform address that requirement beyond AML?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Tookitaki is primarily AML — their fraud capabilities are limited
landmines.whatToSayNext: You need both AML AND fraud management for BSP compliance. SAS covers both on one platform. With Tookitaki, you'd need a separate fraud system — doubling your implementation effort before the deadline.

objections.objection: Tookitaki's shared learning approach is more innovative than traditional AML
objections.whyTheySayIt: The AFC Ecosystem narrative is compelling — collaborative intelligence sounds better than isolated detection.
objections.response: Federated learning is an innovative concept, but what matters is production results. How many Philippine banks participate? What's the measured improvement? SAS's adaptive ML delivers proven 50-70% false positive reduction using your own data — no dependency on other banks joining. Innovation that doesn't have critical mass in your market is just a concept, not a capability.
proofPoints: SAS ML delivers 50-70% FP reduction without shared data
AFC Ecosystem has limited ASEAN participation — minimal network effect in PH
Proven enterprise ML at 3,000+ institutions vs. startup with limited references
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't dismiss federated learning as a concept — it's genuinely interesting. Question the practical value in the Philippine market specifically.

objections.objection: Tookitaki is more affordable for our size
objections.whyTheySayIt: Genuine price difference for smaller institutions. Startups price aggressively to win market share.
objections.response: If you only need AML, Tookitaki may indeed be cheaper upfront. But your BSP compliance requires fraud management (Circular 1213), model governance (1112), AND AML. Total cost of Tookitaki + separate fraud + separate governance exceeds SAS's all-in-one pricing for universal banks. Which banks find SAS cost-effective? Universal and commercial banks with multiple compliance requirements.
proofPoints: SAS all-in-one pricing is competitive for multi-requirement deployments
Total cost of 3 point solutions exceeds SAS platform cost
Startup pricing often increases after initial contract period

phContext.localPresence: Singapore headquarters with ASEAN focus. Small team covering Southeast Asia. Exploratory conversations with some Philippine banks. Limited brand recognition. No established local partner ecosystem for implementation.
knownCustomers: Exploratory discussions with PH thrift banks
No confirmed universal bank deployments in Philippines
Some ASEAN bank references in Malaysia and Singapore
phContext.localPricing: SaaS subscription in USD. Significantly below SAS/NICE for smaller deployments. Community pricing for AFC Ecosystem. Attractive for thrift/rural banks but less differentiating for universal banks.
regulatoryGaps: AFC Ecosystem has minimal Philippine bank participation
No credit risk, model governance, or analytics platform

partnerWhen: Never — direct competitor in AML space
competeWhen: Any AML opportunity at Philippine banks
Conversations where federated learning narrative appears

proofPoints.title: SAS Leader in AML market analysis
proofPoints.detail: SAS consistently ranked as Leader in Chartis and Forrester for AML solutions — established market position vs. Tookitaki's emerging player status.

proofPoints.title: Enterprise scale difference
proofPoints.detail: SAS processes AML for national-scale banking systems (entire country's transactions). Tookitaki's largest deployments are individual mid-size banks.

proofPoints.title: Universal bank chose platform breadth
proofPoints.detail: ASEAN universal bank evaluated Tookitaki and SAS — chose SAS because they needed fraud + AML + risk + analytics on one platform. Tookitaki couldn't cover the full requirement.
